description:
## Parent

bsm-7en — Render selected issue details in the issue explorer

## Background and intent

After bsm-7en.1, Beadsmith has a canonical Issue contract loaded from the Current Workspace through the existing `bw list --all --json` → Rust adapter → TauRPC/Specta → Effect → React path. The next user-visible gap is interaction: the right pane still acts like a placeholder even though the Issue List has enough data to show a selected Issue.

This bead creates the first complete selected-Issue path. It should make the Issue List clickable, keep selection local to React state, and render a useful but intentionally minimal Issue Detail. This gives users a clear sense that the Issue Explorer is now a browser for Issues rather than just a static list.

This bead also introduces the frontend component-test harness needed by later detail slices. Earlier unit tests cover adapter/RPC/Effect behavior; Issue Detail is primarily user-facing UI, so component tests should become the fast feedback seam from this point forward.

## What to build

Make Issue rows accessible clickable controls. Store only the selected Issue ID in React state and derive the selected Issue from the loaded Issue collection. Do not auto-select an Issue when the list loads. When no Issue is selected, the right pane should show the empty detail state. Clicking an Issue selects it, highlights that row, and renders a basic Issue Detail view in the right pane. Clicking the already-selected Issue keeps it selected; it must not toggle back to empty.

The basic Issue Detail should show only the top-level identifying/scanning information:

- title
- de-emphasized Issue ID beside the title
- status
- priority displayed as `P#`
- type
- labels as compact chips when labels are present

Empty labels should be hidden. Markdown description, dependency cards, optional metadata, and comments are handled by later beads.

If React Testing Library / jsdom / jest-dom setup is not already present, add the smallest project-level test setup needed to support component tests for this and later Issue Detail slices.

## Reasoning and constraints

- Keep selection local to React state for this epic. Do not add URL, route, history, persistence, or global state.
- Store the selected Issue ID, not the whole Issue object, so selection remains derived from the current loaded Issue collection.
- Do not add keyboard Up/Down navigation or Escape-to-deselect behavior. The row semantics should support future keyboard work without implementing that broader interaction now.
- Keep the layout consistent with the existing high-density dark developer-tool UI. Avoid decorative padding or chrome that does not help Issue reading.
- Keep Issue List and Issue Detail as separate view presentations over the same Issue data. Do not introduce an `IssueDetail` RPC/domain type.

## Testing guidance

Component tests are the primary fast feedback seam for this bead. Tests should assert user-visible behavior: what is shown before selection, what changes after clicking, and what stays selected after a repeat click. Avoid testing internal component structure.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] React state stores only the selected Issue ID and derives the selected Issue from the loaded Issue collection.
- [ ] No Issue is auto-selected on load.
- [ ] The detail pane shows the specified empty state until an Issue is selected.
- [ ] Issue rows are accessible clickable controls.
- [ ] Clicking an Issue row selects it and renders its basic Issue Detail.
- [ ] The selected row is visually highlighted.
- [ ] Clicking the already-selected Issue keeps it selected and does not clear the detail pane.
- [ ] The basic detail view shows de-emphasized ID beside title, status, `P#` priority, type, and labels when present.
- [ ] Empty labels are hidden.
- [ ] Component test infrastructure is available if it was not already present.
- [ ] Unit/component tests cover empty detail state, selecting an Issue, selected-row highlighting, repeat-click behavior, basic metadata, and label omission/display.
- [ ] Existing list-loading and list-rendering tests continue to pass under the Issue terminology introduced in bsm-7en.1.

## Out of scope

- No Markdown description rendering.
- No dependency card.
- No other metadata section.
- No comments section.
- No automatic selection of the first Issue.
- No deselect behavior.
- No keyboard Up/Down navigation.
- No route, URL, history, or persisted selected Issue state.
- No E2E expansion in this bead.

## Blocked by

bsm-7en.1

comments.text:
# Implementation Plan

## Goal
Make the Issue List select an Issue and render a minimal, read-only Issue Detail pane while adding the smallest React component-test harness needed to verify the interaction.

## Tasks
1. **Add React component-test dependencies and setup**: Install the minimal testing stack for DOM component tests.
   - File: `package.json`
   - Changes: Add dev dependencies for `@testing-library/react`, `@testing-library/jest-dom`, `@testing-library/user-event`, and `jsdom`.
   - File: `pnpm-lock.yaml`
   - Changes: Update lockfile via `pnpm install` after editing dependencies.
   - File: `vitest.config.ts`
   - Changes: Set Vitest `test.environment` to `"jsdom"` and add `setupFiles: ["src/test/setup.ts"]` while preserving the existing `e2e/**` exclusion.
   - New file: `src/test/setup.ts`
   - Changes: Import `@testing-library/jest-dom/vitest` so DOM matchers are available to component tests.
   - Acceptance: `pnpm test` can run existing `.test.ts` unit tests and new `.test.tsx` component tests without environment errors.

2. **Extract the issue explorer presentation into a testable React component**: Separate loading from UI state so component tests can render deterministic Issue collections.
   - File: `src/App.tsx`
   - Changes: Keep only the TauRPC loading state/effect in `App`, then render a new `IssueExplorer` component with `issueState={issueState}`.
   - New file: `src/issues/issue-explorer.tsx`
   - Changes: Move the existing sidebar, issue list, row rendering, empty/loading/failure states, and detail pane layout into exported React components/helpers as needed. Keep existing terminology: Issue, Issue List, Issue Detail, Current Workspace.
   - Acceptance: The app still renders the current sidebar/list behavior for loading, failure, empty, and success states before selection logic is added.

3. **Add local selected-Issue state derived by ID**: Implement selection without introducing global state, routes, persistence, or a new RPC/domain type.
   - File: `src/issues/issue-explorer.tsx`
   - Changes: Add `const [selectedIssueId, setSelectedIssueId] = useState<string | null>(null);`. Derive `selectedIssue` from the loaded `IssueLoadState` collection with `find((issue) => issue.id === selectedIssueId)`. Do not store the whole Issue object. Do not select the first Issue automatically. If the selected ID is absent from the current collection, render the empty detail state.
   - Acceptance: On initial successful load the right pane remains in the empty state, and only clicking an Issue row changes the detail pane.

4. **Make Issue rows accessible clickable controls**: Convert each Issue row from a static article into a selectable control while keeping dense list styling.
   - File: `src/issues/issue-explorer.tsx`
   - Changes: Update `IssueRow` to accept `isSelected` and `onSelect` props. Render a full-width native `<button type="button">` (inside the existing `<li>`) with the current row content, `aria-label` including ID/title/metadata, and `aria-current="true"` when selected. Use `onClick={() => onSelect(issue.id)}` so clicking the selected row keeps it selected rather than toggling off.
   - Changes: Add selected-row visual styling, e.g. a subtle accent border/background consistent with the existing dark high-density UI.
   - Acceptance: Rows are reachable and activatable as buttons; selected rows have an accessible selected/current state and a visible highlight; repeat-clicking the selected row does not clear selection.

5. **Render the basic Issue Detail view**: Replace the placeholder right pane with an empty state or the selected Issue’s basic identifying metadata.
   - File: `src/issues/issue-explorer.tsx`
   - Changes: Add an `IssueDetailPane`/`IssueDetailContent` component that receives `selectedIssue?: Issue`. When absent, render the empty detail state with the current icon/title but remove outdated copy saying details are not implemented. When present, render only:
     - title
     - de-emphasized Issue ID beside the title
     - status
     - priority as `P#`
     - type
     - labels as compact chips when non-empty
   - Changes: Reuse `toIssueViewModel(issue)` for normalized title, ID, status label, `P#` priority, type label, and filtered labels. Do not render Markdown description, dependencies, comments, routes, history, or deselect controls.
   - Acceptance: The detail pane shows the requested fields for the selected Issue, hides empty labels, and remains visually aligned with the existing dark developer-tool UI.

6. **Add component tests for the selection/detail slice**: Cover user-visible behavior through React Testing Library.
   - New file: `src/issues/issue-explorer.test.tsx`
   - Changes: Build small `Issue` fixtures and render `IssueExplorer` with success states. Use `@testing-library/react`, `@testing-library/user-event`, and `within` scoped to the right-pane `main`/`aria-label="Issue detail"` region to avoid confusing list-row text with detail text.
   - Test cases:
     1. Successful Issue List render shows the empty detail state before any click; no Issue is auto-selected.
     2. Clicking an Issue row renders the selected Issue title, de-emphasized ID, status, `P#` priority, type, and label chips in the detail pane.
     3. The clicked row is marked/highlighted as selected.
     4. Clicking the already-selected row again leaves the detail pane populated and keeps the row selected.
     5. Detail labels are omitted when the selected Issue has no non-empty labels and displayed when labels are present.
   - Acceptance: `pnpm test` fails without the selection/detail implementation and passes after it.

7. **Keep existing view-model and loader tests passing**: Ensure the new component uses the current Issue contract instead of changing backend/RPC semantics.
   - File: `src/issues/issue-view.ts`
   - Changes: Prefer no changes. Only add a detail-specific helper if it removes duplication without altering existing list view semantics.
   - File: `src/issues/issue-view.test.ts`
   - Changes: Prefer no changes unless a helper is added; keep existing row metadata expectations intact.
   - File: `src/issues/issue-loader.test.ts`
   - Changes: No planned changes.
   - Acceptance: Existing unit tests continue to pass under `pnpm test`.

8. **Run validation**: Verify frontend behavior and code quality without widening scope into E2E.
   - Files: All modified/new frontend files.
   - Commands: Run `pnpm test`, `pnpm check`, and `pnpm build`.
   - Acceptance: All three commands pass. Do not add or modify WebDriver E2E tests for this bead.

## Files to Modify
- `package.json` - add the smallest React Testing Library/jsdom/jest-dom/user-event dev dependency set.
- `pnpm-lock.yaml` - lock dependency changes from `pnpm install`.
- `vitest.config.ts` - configure jsdom and the component-test setup file while preserving E2E exclusion.
- `src/App.tsx` - leave TauRPC loading/effect wiring here and delegate rendering to the new issue explorer component.
- `src/issues/issue-view.ts` - only if a small detail helper is necessary; otherwise leave unchanged.
- `src/issues/issue-view.test.ts` - only if `issue-view.ts` changes; otherwise leave unchanged.

## New Files
- `src/test/setup.ts` - Vitest setup importing `@testing-library/jest-dom/vitest`.
- `src/issues/issue-explorer.tsx` - testable Issue Explorer presentation, row selection state, clickable rows, and basic Issue Detail pane.
- `src/issues/issue-explorer.test.tsx` - component tests for empty detail, selection, highlighting, repeat-click behavior, metadata rendering, and label display/omission.

## Dependencies
- Task 1 must happen before Task 6 because component tests need jsdom and DOM matchers.
- Task 2 should happen before Tasks 3-6 so the UI can be tested without mocking TauRPC.
- Task 3 depends on Task 2 because selected ID state should live in the testable explorer component.
- Task 4 depends on Task 3 because row controls need to update selected Issue ID.
- Task 5 depends on Task 3 because the detail pane derives from the selected Issue.
- Task 6 depends on Tasks 3-5 so tests can assert complete user-visible behavior.
- Task 8 depends on all implementation and test tasks.

## Risks
- `vitest.config.ts` changing the default environment to jsdom could affect existing non-DOM unit tests; this is usually safe, but `pnpm test` must confirm.
- The row highlight test may be brittle if it asserts exact Tailwind classes; prefer asserting `aria-current="true"` plus one stable selected-state class only if visual coverage is needed.
- `@testing-library/*` package versions should be added by `pnpm` instead of hand-editing the lockfile.
- Keep the detail scope strict: no Markdown description, dependency cards, optional metadata, comments, routing, persistence, auto-selection, deselection, or keyboard Up/Down/Escape behavior.
- This planner could not run `bw comment` because no shell/command runner is available in the subagent toolset; the parent must add the bead comment using this plan.

title: Select an Issue and render basic Issue Detail
